chapter_title.en: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations

text: 4306 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ عَلَيَّ بَدَنَةٌ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّ أَيْنَ يَنْحَرُهَا قَالَ "إِنَّمَا اَلنَّحْرُ بِمِنًى يَقْسِمُهَا بَيْنَ اَلْمَسَاكِينِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4306 - Aban narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding a man who said: "I am obligated to offer a camel," but did not specify where it should be slaughtered.
Imam (as) said: "The slaughtering is to be done at Mina, and it should be distributed among the needy."
